summary: China PP rose ~100 USD over 3 days then paused near 1,090-1,100/ton
Jinneng PP yarn/inj 1,095 FOB Qingdao (up 85); ~1,120-1,130 CIF
NSRP 114G quoted above some imports; other PP lines held flat
paras: PP prices in China rose sharply by around USD 100/ton over the first three days of this week, then paused today (16 July), holding around USD 1,090-1,100/ton for homopolymer grades.

The distributor of China's Jinneng today issued PP and BOPP offers on FOB Qingdao (China) terms to Vietnamese buyers, for August 2026 delivery. Accordingly, Jinneng's export offer for PP yarn/injection was USD 1,095/ton FOB, and BOPP was USD 1,105/ton FOB, up sharply by USD 85/ton from the previous week.

Freight from China to Vietnam typically adds USD 20-30/ton over the FOB price, so Jinneng's CIF Vietnam offer for PP homo and BOPP could be around USD 1,120-1,130/ton CIF. In practice, however, amid this week's PP price reversal, many suppliers offered considerably higher. For example, one distributor offered Jinneng's PP yarn/injection/BOPP to Vietnam at USD 1,150-1,160/ton CIF.

One distributor is even offering numerous PP products from China's Yulong to Vietnam at prices far above the general market. Accordingly, this distributor's offers for Yulong's PP yarn/injection, BOPP, and PP copo were USD 1,230-1,240/ton CIF Hai Phong, higher than the aforementioned Jinneng PP offers by USD 80-100/ton.

In domestic trading, PP prices continued to swing sharply. Today, most northern traders offered NSRP's PP yarn 114G and PP injection 105G at up to VND 32,000/kg incl. VAT for customers needing immediate delivery — an increase of VND 500-1,000/kg from yesterday (depending on the supplier). For the same PP 114G but for customers taking delivery in late July/early August, offers were at VND 31,300-31,500/kg incl. VAT.

Notably, PP 114G is currently offered even higher than some imported PP yarn grades or the PP yarn of the two producers BSR and Hyosung Vina. Specifically, one distributor offered Daelim-origin PP yarn UH450J and S-Oil (South Korea)-origin PP yarn HD500 both at VND 31,000/kg incl. VAT, delivered in the north. One distributor offered SCG (Thailand)-origin PP injection P700J at VND 32,500/kg incl. VAT at Hai Phong port.

In the south, the offer price for Hyosung Vina's PP yarn F501 held at VND 31,000/kg incl. VAT, after a sharp rise of VND 1,500-2,000/kg over the past three days (the previous week, the lowest selling price for Hyosung Vina PP yarn was recorded at VND 29,100/kg incl. VAT). IRPC (Thailand)-origin PP Film B1101 (laminating grade) was at VND 31,000/kg incl. VAT.

Aside from the PP homo line, offers for other PP products largely held flat today. The main reason PP copo, Film, and coating offers have not risen in line with the general PP homo trend this cycle is that their price levels remain high.

For instance, for PP coating, Lotte South Korea's M9600 was offered in the south at VND 42,000-43,000/kg incl. VAT. In the north, few sellers hold M9600 stock for immediate delivery, creating a mild shortage. For PP copo, in the north, many traders offered S-Oil (South Korea)-origin PP copo or Chinese-origin PP copo at VND 34,000-35,000/kg incl. VAT; in the south, the common trading level was VND 33,000-34,000/kg incl. VAT.

A positive signal is that, with this week's early-week uptick in resin prices, trading has become more active and buyers are restocking more, helping sellers clear inventory faster and hold higher selling prices rather than being pushed down sharply as before.

summary: Lotte Korea agreed LLDPE Film/inj at 980 USD/ton CIF, below China
Yulong China LLDPE 7042 1,165; HDPE blow 1,180-1,205 USD/ton CIF
Domestic LLDPE Film 7042 raised to 31,500-32,000 VND/kg
paras: As of today, 16 July, Lotte Chemical of South Korea had, earlier this week, agreed to sell LLDPE Film/injection to Vietnamese buyers at USD 980/ton CIF, significantly lower than Chinese-origin material. Today, however, the distributor has temporarily suspended its offers.

Some domestic buyers had even confirmed orders for Lotte's LLDPE at the start of the week, only to be told later that the orders were cancelled. According to one source, Lotte and several other suppliers have been holding back stock, or redirecting PP/PE sales to the Chinese market, where prices can fetch around USD 50-60/ton more than in Vietnam.

Similarly, PE offer prices from the distributor of Russia's Sibur at Hai Phong port fluctuated back and forth this week, rising and falling within a range of VND 100-300/kg versus the previous week, with several HDPE grades reported sold out. Specifically, LLDPE Film was offered at VND 28,600-28,800/kg incl. VAT, while two off-grade HDPE Film/blow grades from Sibur rose to VND 27,700-31,100/kg incl. VAT. Sibur's distributor is reportedly prioritizing offers to the Chinese market, as it yields higher margins than selling to Vietnam.

For Chinese-origin material, one distributor is offering numerous PP products from Yulong to Vietnam, though this distributor's offer prices are considered notably higher than the general market. Yulong's LLDPE 7042 was offered at USD 1,165/ton CIF; HDPE yarn at USD 1,280/ton CIF; and HDPE blow ranged from USD 1,180-1,205/ton CIF, with grade HDPE 5502S offered as high as USD 1,380/ton CIF. Most other suppliers offered Chinese-origin HDPE Film/yarn to Vietnam at USD 1,150-1,200/ton CIF, a sharp increase from the previous week.

For U.S.-origin material, one distributor reported that offer prices for Cynpol PE products to Vietnam were trending up by USD 30-40/ton versus the previous week, owing to rising freight rates. Accordingly, offers for Cynpol's LDPE Film MI 4 rose to USD 1,140-1,150/ton CIF; LLDPE Film 0218H/0218F was offered at USD 930/ton CIF; Cynpol HDPE injection at USD 950/ton CIF; and HDPE Film at USD 980/ton CIF.

In domestic trading today, many traders continued to raise offers for LLDPE Film 7042 of Chinese origin to VND 31,500-32,000/kg incl. VAT in the north, for July-August delivery; Sibur's LLDPE Film 20211FE was also offered up to VND 31,500/kg incl. VAT; and Sabic's LLDPE Film 218WJ saw some sellers raise offers sharply to VND 32,500/kg incl. VAT in the north. Some sellers offered U.S.- and Canada-origin LLDPE Film at VND 30,500/kg incl. VAT, but for distant delivery in September 2026.

For LDPE, some traders offered U.S.-origin LDPE Film at VND 38,000/kg incl. VAT. However, some sellers offered LDPE Radco and LDPE Film of Formosa (Taiwan) and Thailand origin at just VND 36,000/kg incl. VAT.

For HDPE, the distributor of LSP offered CP Chem (Singapore)-origin HDPE blow at VND 35,500-36,100/kg incl. VAT depending on grade; one distributor offered STL (China)-origin HDPE Film FS7000 at VND 35,000/kg incl. VAT; Formosa (Taiwan) HDPE Film 5604F at VND 34,000/kg incl. VAT at Hai Phong port; Lyondell Basell (China)-origin HDPE pipe 23050 (PE100 group) at VND 35,500/kg incl. VAT; and HDPE yarn offers were commonly fluctuating around VND 35,000-36,000/kg incl. VAT.

summary: Platts: China PVC +5 to 750 USD/ton CFR; SE Asia +10 this week
Korea launches anti-dumping probe on suspension-grade PVC imports
paras: According to Platts pricing published today, 16 July, PVC prices in China rose slightly by USD 5/ton this week to USD 750/ton CFR; PVC prices in Southeast Asia rose USD 10/ton versus the previous week to USD 705/ton CFR.

In practice, China's PVC FOB prices rose USD 10-15/ton this week, with oil-based PVC quoted at USD 675/ton FOB and coal-based PVC at USD 670/ton FOB. The price gap between the two PVC lines is not large.

More broadly across the region, Asian PVC prices rose this week, supported by a sharp increase in input feedstock costs, despite regional demand remaining low. Suppliers have not been able to raise PVC prices further to match the feedstock movement, held back by subdued trading. Many coal-based PVC producers have cut operating rates.

Another notable development is that the Korea Trade Commission has launched an anti-dumping investigation into imports of suspension-grade polyvinyl chloride (S-PVC) from China into South Korea. This has a certain impact on China's PVC exports.

Back in the Vietnamese market, PVC offers from distributors largely held flat this week rather than showing the clear upward reversal seen in other resins. This is because domestic producer AGC Vina (ACVN) just issued separate quotes to some large customers seeking early pricing on August 2026 PVC shipments, with PVC PM66R at only VND 18,800/kg excl. VAT (roughly VND 20,300/kg with the full 8% VAT), a sharp drop of VND 1,500/kg versus the July shipment offer published in mid-June.
In the commercial market, PVC offer and trading levels are commonly around VND 20,000-21,500/kg incl. VAT.
